% Four identical pages differing only in the page /Rotate attribute:
% page 1 = /Rotate 0 (control), page 2 = 90, page 3 = 180, page 4 = 270.
% The content stream is byte-identical portrait text on every page, so any
% difference in extracted geometry between pages is purely the /Rotate term.
\documentclass[12pt]{article}
\usepackage[paperwidth=8.5in,paperheight=11in,margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage[hidelinks]{hyperref}
\pagestyle{empty}
\setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
\begin{document}

\newcommand{\testpage}{%
MARKER

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This paragraph exists so
that line detection has several horizontal text lines to cluster. Each
line should be recovered as a distinct band regardless of the page
rotation attribute, once coordinates are normalised.

Final line of the test page.%
}

\pdfpageattr{/Rotate 0}
\testpage

% Internal link from the upright page to a target on the /Rotate 90 page,
% so link-destination page-point resolution is exercised across rotations.
\medskip
\hyperlink{target90}{LINKTEXT}
\clearpage

\pdfpageattr{/Rotate 90}
\hypertarget{target90}{}%
\testpage
\clearpage

\pdfpageattr{/Rotate 180}
\testpage
\clearpage

\pdfpageattr{/Rotate 270}
\testpage

\end{document}
